title: Why Grit Matters More Than Talent
subtitle: 天赋不如努力：毅力才是成功的关键

paragraphs.en: With those born with natural talents, it feels as if they excel without really trying. But what about those of us who don't have a natural talent? We've been told all our lives that if you work hard, you too can succeed. But with the release of Angela Duckworth's Grit, we are given a new key to success. 'As much as talent counts, effort counts twice,' says Duckworth in Grit.
paragraphs.zh: 对于那些天赋异禀的人，我们总觉得他们毫不费力就能出类拔萃。但我们这些没有天赋的人呢？我们一生都被灌输：只要努力，也能成功。而随着安吉拉·达克沃斯《坚毅》一书的出版，我们获得了一把通往成功的新钥匙。达克沃斯在书中写道：“天赋固然重要，但努力的重要性是它的两倍。”

paragraphs.en: She introduces a new concept that talent may be overrated, and if you want real success, what you need is grit, the perfect combination of passion and persistence. Even if you have natural talent, it's nothing without grit. Duckworth says grit is the difference between success and failure. A person who has grit is more likely to succeed than a person who does not. When we think about attaining success — whether it's landing that job or learning that new skill — our thoughts are immediately burdened by all the things we must first learn.
paragraphs.zh: 她提出了一个新概念：天赋或许被高估了，若想获得真正的成功，你需要的是坚毅——激情与坚持的完美结合。即便你天赋异禀，若无坚毅也毫无意义。达克沃斯认为，坚毅是成功与失败的分水岭。有毅力的人比没有毅力的人更可能成功。当我们思考如何取得成功——无论是找到一份工作还是学习一项新技能——脑海中立刻充斥着我们首先必须学习的所有东西。

paragraphs.en: If you want that new job, you have to learn the job skills, then the interview skills, then the dress part — and you must be perfect at all of them. Grit is different because it tells us that perfection isn't the goal. Grit lifts the unreasonable expectations off our shoulders. Grit tells us that the door is open wider than we first thought possible. Grit allows us to redefine our goals.
paragraphs.zh: 想得到那份新工作，你得先学工作技能，再学面试技巧，然后还得学会穿搭——并且必须样样精通。坚毅则不同，它告诉我们完美并非目标。坚毅将不切实际的期望从我们肩上卸下。它告诉我们，机会之门比我们最初想象的敞得更开。坚毅让我们得以重新定义自己的目标。

paragraphs.en: Think about it: what's something you've always wanted to do, but gave up because you don't have the skills for it? What's something you love but aren't good at? The real workings of grit are to have sustainable passion and continue to try. Effort means more than your natural ability. Even if you haven't mastered a skill, grit tells you that you can still succeed if you can transform your passion into action. In a way, Duckworth is giving new hope to people who have shut the doors on their dreams. She is saying it is possible that you can accomplish anything. If at first you fail, then try one more time with grit.
paragraphs.zh: 想一想：有什么是你一直想做却因“没那本事”而放弃的？有什么是你喜爱却不擅长的？坚毅的真正内涵在于拥有持久的热情并不断尝试。努力比天赋更重要。即便你尚未掌握某项技能，坚毅也告诉你，只要能将激情转化为行动，你仍能成功。在某种程度上，达克沃斯为那些对梦想关上大门的人带来了新希望。她是在说，你有可能做成任何事。如果一开始失败了，那就带着坚毅再试一次。
